extraction.one_line_summary: OLDANI Stefano won from a 25-rider breakaway that finally escaped after 70km of 55kph racing, outsprinting ROTA Lorenzo after late attacks.
tactical_patterns: OLDANI and ROTA launched definitive move on penultimate climb with 50km remaining, building 1-minute gap through sustained cooperation.
Breakaway formation delayed until 70km despite 55kph opening hour suggests peloton aggressively controlled early terrain near Parma.
BUITRAGO and HAMILTON chased at 20km but couldn't close 40-second gap, revealing GC teams unwilling to fully commit domestiques.
OLDANI attacked twice in final kilometer despite superior sprint speed, indicating lack of confidence or tactical misdirection attempt.

rider_signals.OLDANI Stefano: positive: Won first professional race from long breakaway, then showed fastest sprint despite tactical inexperience in finale.
rider_signals.ROTA Lorenzo: positive: Initiated winning move on penultimate climb, sustained cooperation for 50km despite losing sprint.
rider_signals.BUITRAGO Santiago: neutral: Chased leaders inside 20km with HAMILTON but couldn't bridge 40-second gap for time gains.
rider_signals.HAMILTON Lucas: neutral: Took 8 minutes on GC from late chase group but couldn't reach stage podium.
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: Gained 8 minutes while best-placed GC rider in break at 11 minutes down, smart positioning for mountain stages.
rider_signals.GABBURO Davide: positive: Bridged with COVILI to 25-rider break after missing initial split, showing persistence in high-speed chase.
rider_signals.COVILI Luca: positive: Successfully chased down 25-rider breakaway alongside GABBURO after initial Bahrain missed the move.
